title: Fetch and compare deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Fetch and compare; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Fetch and compare: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Fetching is the same as pulling and can silently rewrite the current branch's files.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Fetch updates the local view of remote references only; inspect divergence and choose a separate integration operation deliberately.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Fetch updates the local view of remote references only; inspect divergence and choose a separate integration operation deliberately.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Fetch and compare instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Fetch updates the local view of remote references only; inspect divergence and choose a separate integration operation deliberately.
Fetching is the same as pulling and can silently rewrite the current branch's files.

answer.explanation: Fetch updates the local view of remote references only; inspect divergence and choose a separate integration operation deliberately.
